result.summary: Amazon exposes multiple HTTP developer APIs—App Submission, Appstore Reporting, Vitals, Data Portability, plus Amazon Pay and Incentives surfaces—with auth via Login with Amazon security-profile tokens (Bearer), Data Portability LWA OAuth tokens, Amazon Pay signed requests using a publicKeyId/private key pair (optionally plus delegated auth token), or Incentives Partner ID + access/secret keys.

amazon_dev_security_profile.label: Amazon Developer Console security profile (client ID + client secret for LWA client-credentials APIs)
amazon_dev_security_profile.setup: Sign in to the [Amazon Developer Console](https://developer.amazon.com/login.html), go to **My Settings → API Access**, choose the API, create a new security profile, attach it to that API, then copy the `Client ID` and `Client Secret` from the profile's **Web Settings** tab. Use them to mint a Login with Amazon access token at `https://api.amazon.com/auth/o2/token` with `grant_type=client_credentials` and the documented scope for the API, such as `appstore::apps:readwrite` or `adx_reporting::appstore:marketer`.

amazon_lwa_profile.label: Login with Amazon security profile (client ID + optional client secret for OAuth authorization-code apps)
amazon_lwa_profile.setup: In the [Amazon Developer Console](https://developer.amazon.com/), open **Login with Amazon**, choose **Create a New Security Profile**, fill in the profile details, and save. Then use **Show Client ID and Client Secret** to retrieve the OAuth credentials. Configure your redirect URI as documented, get any required allowlisting for the product, then exchange the authorization code for tokens at the regional `auth/o2/token` endpoint such as `https://api.amazon.com/auth/o2/token`.

amazon_incentives_keys.label: Amazon Incentives API access credentials (Partner ID, access key, secret key)
amazon_incentives_keys.setup: Contact Amazon through the [Amazon Incentives contact page](https://www.amazon-incentives.com/contact-us) to obtain a `Partner ID`, then sign in to the [Amazon Incentives API Portal](https://developer.amazon.com/docs/incentives-api/incentives-portal.html). Use the portal's security credentials page to create and manage your API access keys. The docs state the integration uses `PartnerId`, `Access Key`, and `Secret Key`.

amazon_pay_keypair.label: Amazon Pay API key pair (publicKeyId + private key)
amazon_pay_keypair.setup: Set up an [Amazon Pay merchant account](https://pay.amazon.com/) and follow the Amazon Pay integration setup in [Get set up for integration](https://developer.amazon.com/docs/amazon-pay-checkout/get-set-up-for-integration.html). Generate or retrieve your Amazon Pay API keys so you have a `publicKeyId` and corresponding private key. Sign each request with the private key as documented in [Signing requests](https://developer.amazon.com/docs/amazon-pay-api-v2/v1-signing-requests.html); the resulting signature and `PublicKeyId` go into the `Authorization` header.

amazon_pay_auth_token.label: Amazon Pay delegated authorization token
amazon_pay_auth_token.setup: For delegated Amazon Pay calls, first obtain an Amazon Pay delegated authorization token via the documented authorization-token flow, then send it in the `X-Amz-Pay-AuthToken` header together with the normal signed Amazon Pay request. The token is used only for delegated requests on behalf of another party.

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Choose one of the documented regional hosts: `eu-west-1`, `us-east-1`, or `us-west-2`.
variables.description: Regional intake host prefix from the API reference.

variables.name: scopeId
variables.resolveFrom: Your authorized Amazon Data Portability LWA scope ID.
